items.title: Should Investors Be Worried About Google?
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Investors are worried about Google's rampant AI spending. But WSJ's Asa Fitch breaks down why the company's prospects are still solid.

#Google #AI #WSJ</article>

items.title: How Ukraine Is Stopping 90% of Russia's Drones | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>As Russia launches its largest aerial attacks of the war, overwhelming Ukrainian defenses with thousands of one-way attack drones and hundreds of advanced missiles, clearing the sky has become a key priority.

Four years into the war, Ukraine has developed its expertise in dealing with waves of Shahed-type drones, using machine guns, helicopters and electronic warfare to destroy them. Now, as Kyiv appeals to allies for Patriot interceptors to shoot down missiles, it’s leaning on cheap drones to defend cities from drone swarms.

WSJ got exclusive access to film the teams of pilots and navigators in the Lasar’s Group of the National Guard of Ukraine who are pivoting from attacks on Russian heavy equipment to hunting their drones.

Chapters:
0:00 Ukraine’s Lasar’s Group
1:36 Ukraine’s new interceptors for Russian drones
3:19 Prepping and deploying interceptors
5:51 The challenges 
8:23 Watching a test flight
10:36 AI targeting 

WSJ Originals features in-depth reporting, investigations and on-the-ground journalism that uncovers the forces shaping business, politics and the world around us.

#Russia #Ukraine #WSJ</article>

items.title: How This 24-Year-Old AI Whiz Built a $20B Hedge Fund
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Twenty-four-year-old Leopold Aschenbrenner has gone viral for his AI-focused hedge fund, now worth over $20 billion.

The fund has attracted retail investors—and Wall Street's elite alike—and its stock picks have even amassed a cult-like following.

#AI #Investing #WSJ</article>

items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Past efforts to fix Penn Station have broken the hearts of architects and politicians. Thanks to a new design and financing approach—and a real-estate developer in the White House—a plan is on track.⁠

#PennStation #NewYork #WSJ</article>

items.title: How Canada's Geography Makes Its Wildfire Crisis Worse
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content: <article>As hundreds of wildfires rage across Canada, WSJ’s Amanda Coletta explains how the country’s unique geography and worsening climate crisis leave the country in an increasingly volatile position.</article>

items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>New U.K. Prime Minister Andy Burnham rose through the ranks of the Labour party to become Prime Minister without winning a single General Election. So how did he do it?

items.title: Costco's Secret Weapon? Its Millionaire Employees
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Long-tenured workers are Costco’s secret weapon. ⁠
⁠
They are reliable and experienced, able to speed shoppers through a checkout line, and serve as mentors to newer workers, passing down the company’s unique culture, Costco executives say.⁠

#Costco #Grocery #WSJ</article>

items.title: U.S. Emergency Oil Reserve Hits Lowest Levels Since 1983: Why It Matters
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Oil shocks from the war with Iran and the Russia-Ukraine conflict have left the U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve at its lowest level since 1983. WSJ’s David Uberti explains how we got here.

#Oil #Iran #WSJ</article>

items.title: Why NFL Stadiums Aren't Built for the World Cup | WSJ Pro Perfected
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>As the 2026 FIFA World Cup comes to a close with the final between Spain and Argentina at New York New Jersey Stadium, WSJ asked an architect what actually makes a great soccer stadium. Small design details, like the sightline or the stadium’s roof, can affect both players’ performance on the field and fans’ experience in the stands.

Chapters:
0:00 North American World Cup stadiums
0:30 The problem
1:23 The pitch – Kansas City
2:23 The players – Dallas
3:19 The fans – Mexico City
5:25 The final at MetLife Stadium
6:34 What the ultimate stadium looks like

Pro Perfected
Experts in engineering and design break down a ubiquitous problem, examining how the world is built and what can make it better.

#FIFA #WorldCup #WSJ</article>

items.title: Why Microsoft's $80B Xbox Bet Backfired | WSJ What Went Wrong
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Microsoft’s Xbox bet on a subscription service called Game Pass after struggling to keep up in the console market. The company spent more than $80 billion to bring major franchises like “Call of Duty” and “Fallout” onto the platform. Now Xbox is laying off about 3,200 employees and divesting five game studios, with CEO Asha Sharma calling for a “reset” of the business.

WSJ explores what went wrong, and what the future holds for fan-favorite franchises.

Chapters:
0:00 Microsoft’s $80 billion Xbox bet
0:44 The rise of Game Pass
2:16 The pandemic boom
3:20 The fall 
4:51 Xbox’s restructuring

What Went Wrong explores the challenging conditions and decisions that led to a company's downturn.

#Microsoft #Xbox #WSJ</article>

items.title: The Race to Commercialize the World’s Most Powerful Psychedelic | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>An obscure and extremely powerful psychedelic drug called 5-MeO-DMT, or BUFO, is drawing intense interest from the pharmaceutical industry, where biotech companies like AtaiBeckley and GH Research are racing to turn it into a commercially available treatment for severe mental health disorders, like depression, anxiety and PTSD. 

WSJ took an inside look at how the psychedelic is being used at unregulated clinics in Mexico, and why companies are betting that it could soon turn into a billion dollar blockbuster drug.  

Chapters:
0:00 The power of 5-MeO-DMT
1:31 Inside a treatment facility in Mexico
5:36 5-MeO-DMT uses and history
8:10 The psychedelic therapies market
10:46 The race to commercialize psychedelics
14:08 After the treatment in Mexico

WSJ Originals features in-depth reporting, investigations and on-the-ground journalism that uncovers the forces shaping business, politics and the world around us.

#DMT #Psychedlics #WSJ</article>
